title: The Bacteria in Your Gums Can Reach Your Breast Tumors. Scientists Have Finally Mapped How.
description: A 2026 Johns Hopkins study has traced the molecular route by which a common gum disease bacterium travels through the bloodstream, docks onto breast cancer cells, and accelerates both tumor growth and metastasis — with especially dangerous effects in women carrying BRCA1 mutations. The mechanism is now mapped in extraordinary detail. The clinical implications remain fiercely contested. Here's what the evidence actually says.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for The Bacteria in Your Gums Can Reach Your Breast Tumors. Scientists Have Finally Mapped How.
